rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STAN KWONG IRREVOCABLE TRUST II
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Case To Trial
rulings.Rulings: Respondent's Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Bring Case to Trial is granted under CCP § 583.360. The statutory time limit in which to commence trial on this petition was extended by written stipulation to no later than June 26, 2017, and potentially by oral stipulation on the record to September 27, 2017. The plain meaning of the last stipulation between the parties continuing the trial date does not expressly waive Respondent's right to bring a motion to dismiss, nor does it extend the statutory time limit indefinitely. Petitioner has not met the burden to show by clear and convincing evidence that the statutory time limit was further tolled due to impossibility, futility, or impracticality. Petitioner has not shown that she acted with due diligence to bring this case to trial at all stages of the proceeding; Petitioner's papers show that she was aware that the extended statutory time limit in which to commence trial had run, and yet did not seek to specially set the petition for trial until after this Motion to Dismiss was filed. There is insufficient showing that Respondent is equitably estopped from bringing this Motion to Dismiss. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 8, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STAN KWONG IRREVOCABLE TRUST II
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Order Setting Trial Date is denied, for the same reasons set forth in the tentative ruling on the concurrently-heard Motion to Dismiss. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 8, 2018.

Once an Amended Petition has been filed, Examiner will conduct a full review.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Conduct Mental Examination Of Conservatee Donald M. Wilson To C.C.P 2030
rulings.Rulings: Respondents are entitled to a mental examination of conservatee Donald M. Wilson pursuant to CCP § 2032.010, et seq. However, Conservator's request that the examiners be disallowed from asking Mr. Wilson about the alleged transaction(s) between Mr. Wilson and Respondents is granted. That information can first be obtained from a deposition of Mr. Wilson where his attorney will be present to object to any improper questions. There is no showing of good cause at this time to allow this inquiry in the context of a mental examination to determine Mr. Wilson's capacity and susceptibility to undue influence. After the examiners have reviewed the deposition, they may renew their request and have the opportunity to show why further questioning over the details of the transaction(s) are necessary in order for them to reach an opinion concerning Mr. Wilson's cognitive capacity and susceptibility to undue influence. Conservator's request that Respondents be barred from communicating with Mr. Wilson for three weeks prior to each examination will be discussed in the context of the Petition to Terminate Visits. Accordingly, the exam will proceed in two sessions. Mr. Wilson shall appear on May 30, 2018 at 8:30 a.m. at 2000 Van Ness Ave., Suite 512, in San Francisco, CA for testing administered by Dr. Ronald Roberts. Mr. Wilson shall also appear on June 1, 2018 at 10:00 a.m. at 655 Redwood Highway, Mill Valley, CA for a psychiatric examination by Dr. Mark Levy. The context of the entire mental examination shall be recorded by audio technology, pursuant to CCP § 2032.530. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 8, 2018.
